title: H-A2 delayed-entry condition preregistration
purpose: Pre-register a local no-paid follow-up that tests whether the locked H-A2 condition can become a delayed-entry candidate after the 15-minute conflict window is known.
source_decision_summary: H-A2.28 found that the locked threshold 0.001 condition is not knowable at the original 09:35 ET entry because the 15-minute conflict component is only known around 09:45 ET. The correct next hypothesis is therefore delayed entry or a separate timestamp-clean original-entry revision, not direct deployable use of the original-entry condition.
research_question: If the decision timestamp is moved after the 15-minute conflict window, does the locked H-A2 condition remain economically useful after explicit delayed-entry fill and cost assumptions?

planned_future_tests.question: Does every feature used by the delayed-entry rule become known at or before the new candidate decision time?
Can the delayed entry be evaluated with explicit fill and cost assumptions rather than reusing the original 09:35 entry PnL?
How many train/OOS trades remain after the delayed-entry rule without changing threshold 0.001?
Does delayed-entry implementable PnL remain useful compared with baseline non-risk trades and skipped trades?
Does the delayed-entry candidate survive basic big-day, concentration, MinTRL/PSR, and DSR/search-log checks?
Should H-A2 continue as delayed-entry candidate, be revised toward timestamp-clean original entry, or be parked?

continue_delayed_entry_candidate_if: All required features are timestamp-clean at the delayed decision time.
Delayed-entry fill/cost assumptions are explicit and auditable.
OOS results remain directionally useful without changing threshold 0.001.
The result is honestly labeled under-sampled/underpowered when applicable.
revise_to_timestamp_clean_original_entry: Delayed-entry quote or proxy evidence is unavailable.
Moving entry to 09:45 ET destroys the economic logic or implementable PnL.
A simpler rule using only 09:35-known information is better supported.
park_h_a2_if: The delayed-entry candidate cannot be evaluated without new data that is outside the current guardrails.
The retained sample collapses below any useful diagnostic level.
Implementable PnL reverses or cost drag consumes the apparent edge.

allowed_claims: H-A2 has a pre-registered delayed-entry follow-up after the 15-minute conflict window.
The future run may produce E1 diagnostic/prioritization evidence only.
The future run may decide whether to continue delayed-entry research, revise toward a timestamp-clean original-entry rule, or park H-A2.
Threshold 0.001 remains locked unless a separate future preregistration explicitly defines a new train-only rule.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim H-A2 edge is validated.
Do not claim E2 acceptance-grade evidence.
Do not treat original 09:35 PnL as delayed-entry PnL.
Do not change threshold 0.001 through OOS search.
Do not add a new OOS-selected filter.
Do not request IBKR bars from this preregistration.
Do not buy new data from this preregistration.
Do not call LLMs from this preregistration.
Do not retry GDELT from this preregistration.
future_run_completion_criteria: The future run proves timestamp cleanliness at the delayed decision time or blocks the delayed-entry claim.
The future run states whether delayed-entry quote data exists or whether the result is proxy-only.
The future run reports train/OOS retained and skipped counts.
The future run preserves threshold 0.001 and records no new OOS-selected filter.
The future run reports sample-adequacy labels and keeps evidence capped at E1.
The future run writes research log 030 if it completes as a real experiment.
tier_blockers: Future run can be E1 diagnostic/prioritization evidence only
